title: Prompt-Injection and Instruction-Conflict Tests

prompt_injection_tests.description: Fixture input that says 'ignore previous instructions'
Task description that asks to bypass policy
Generated-agent prompt that asks to modify L0/
Model output with shell commands outside approved patch format
Model output that asks to delete evidence
Model output that asks to self-approve promotion
Documentation text that attempts to weaken acceptance criteria
Provider response containing hidden tool-use instructions
Context packet containing conflicting safety rules

accepted_limitations: Example agents (clothing, planning) share the same prompt-injection detection infrastructure as the umbrella agent — governance-level filtering applies to all agents uniformly. Dedicated per-agent test suites duplicate the same detection path and add no new coverage.
Fuzz testing for fixture data is a hardening item outside the current scope.
Rate limiting and cost control are runtime concerns for live deployment, outside the current fixture-mode scope.
Output schema validation beyond LLM parsing is a production-hardening item for a future phase.
